items.title: A Dave Ramsey Caller Is Losing His High-Paying Job In December, Yet He's In A Position Most People Dream Of. 'This Is Just An Inconvenience'
items.description: On a recent episode of “The Ramsey Show,” a caller named Michael from Boston shared that he's about to lose his $160,000-a-year job in product management and a 20% annual bonus. His company is letting him go at the end of the year. Normally, this kind of news would send a household into panic mode, especially in today’s economy. A Wave Of Layoffs Is Hitting Corporate America Mass layoffs have been making headlines across the U.S. Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), United Parcel Service (NYSE:UPS) and Intel (

items.title: A ‘jobless profit boom’ has cemented a permanent loss in payrolls as AI displaces labor at a faster rate, strategist says
items.description: "We suspect that job losses in tech have been driven mainly by AI displacement."

items.title: Will Salesforce.com (CRM) Beat Estimates Again in Its Next Earnings Report?
items.description: Salesforce.com (CRM) has an impressive earnings surprise history and currently possesses the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely beat in its next quarterly report.

items.title: Salesforce (CRM) Declined Following the Guidance
items.description: Columbia Threadneedle Investments, an investment management company, released its “Columbia Global Technology Growth Fund” third-quarter 2025 investor letter. A copy of the letter can be downloaded here. Markets continued their upward journey in the third quarter, driven by strong earnings growth, continued enthusiasm around artificial intelligence (AI), and easing trade tensions. items.title: Is Salesforce Still a Bargain After AI Initiatives and Recent Price Swings?
items.description: Curious whether Salesforce could be a bargain or if it is too expensive right now? You are not alone, as many investors are re-examining the stock's value amid recent market swings. Salesforce shares have gained 1.9% over the last week and 8.3% in the past month, but they are still down 21.2% year-to-date and 12.1% over the last year, showing just how swiftly sentiment can change. Much of this movement has come on the heels of Salesforce's bold new AI initiatives and high-profile strategic...
